metadata.title:
WITH THE


STATE

DENIES MOTION TO DISCHARGE
FLYNN JURY BECAUSE OF


PUGH INTERVIEW

PUCH GIVES VERSION

ARGUMENT BEFORE JURY
OPENS IN CONSPIRACY


TRIAL

pages.raw_text:
Judge n. L. kennan overruIed
tho motion of the defense for the
discharge of the jury. Prosecutor
Pugh presented affidavits in which
he declared that he was not re-
sponsible for certain articles ap.
pearing in the morning paper re-
garding his fear that the jury
would be intimidated, and that the
interview was written as the re-
suit of private conference with
his deputies, at which the reporter
was present. The defense asked
and was granted an exception.


BLAIR OPENS FOR STATE


Denouncing c. L. Filigno as a
perjurer and Elizabeth Gurley
Flynn Jones as an arch-conspira-
tor, Assistant Corporation Counsel
John e. Blair opened the argument
for the state in the l. w. w. con-
spiracy cases this morning.


DENIES CRIMINAL INTENT


Before you can convict these
defendants" declared Attorney
Thomas j. Symmes in addressing
the jury, you must prove a GRIM
inal intent. In common violation
of ordinances, an intent must not
be proven, but in a criminal case,
the vicious will, the criminal in
tent, must be shown. These I. w.
WIS believed the street speaking
ordinance unconstitutional; they
believed that they were within
their constitutional rights; they
were fighting for a cause.


"The prosecution will tell you of
the Chicago anarchists, of the Hay
market riots, the anarchists of
Russia and other countries; but re-
member this, gentlemen of the
jury, AIT arrests were made during
the recent trouble here and not
man was guilty of violence"


Attorney Symmes' argument was
interrupted by the noon recess. At
1870 o'clock he recommended, to
be followed by Prosecutor Pugh
and Attorney Fred c. Moore.


The crowd which packed the
court room to hear the arguments
was warned by Judge Kennan that
unless that refrained from demon
of any kind the court
room would be cleared.
